title: Complexity, Timing, Profiling, Memory, and Capacity · Contract Workshop
summary: A library-tools pair incrementally builds correctness-preserving capacity study from an original fixed text fixture while retaining earlier Git, Python, Unicode, determinism, bounded-resource, accessibility, testing, and evidence requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of correctness-preserving capacity study while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: State workload, input scale, expected complexity, environment, metric, budget, and correctness invariant before measuring.
Use repeated controlled timing, warmup decisions, robust summaries, and noise disclosure rather than one stopwatch value.
Use cProfile or a justified profiler to locate cumulative work before selecting a repair.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Performance Hypothesis
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Bookbot team handles case 1543 during an assistive-technology terminal review. Build deterministic Python evidence to measure representative time and memory while preserving byte-for-byte correctness; record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: State workload, input scale, expected complexity, environment, metric, budget, and correctness invariant before measuring.

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Performance Regression
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: bookbot-performance-regression-157mrn" that uses case 3887 to measure representative time and memory while preserving byte-for-byte correctness. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-performance-regression-157mrn
# Competency: bookbot-performance-regression.
# Case 3887: an offline public-library reading report.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output.
# Changed case: split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte.
def evidence_bookbot_performance_regression_157mrn(tokens: int = 10000, elapsed_ms: float = 42.0, peak_kib: int = 640, time_budget_ms: float = 100.0, memory_budget_kib: int = 1024, output_equal: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_157mrn = "157mrn-nrm751-3887"
    assert evidence_variant_157mrn
    evidence_axes_157mrn = (
        "corpus157mrn",
        "policynrm751",
        "revision3887",
        "command157mrn3887",
        "reportnrm7513887",
        "test3887157mrn",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_157mrn)) == 6
    if tokens < 1 or elapsed_ms < 0.0 or peak_kib < 0:
        return False, elapsed_ms, "measurement-shape"
    if elapsed_ms > time_budget_ms:
        return False, elapsed_ms, "time-budget"
    if peak_kib > memory_budget_kib:
        return False, peak_kib, "memory-budget"
    if not output_equal:
        return False, elapsed_ms, "semantic-regression"
    assert elapsed_ms <= time_budget_ms
    return True, round(elapsed_ms, 3), "profiled-capacity"

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

Build deterministic Python evidence to measure representative time and memory while preserving byte-for-byte correctness; keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color; then replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Gate representative input slices on correctness first and practical time and memory thresholds second.

For Bookbot and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Performance Regression, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: A faster result may be accepted even if Unicode or ranking output changes.

Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Performance Regression condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
